tossups.question: A John Ashbury cento from his collection Wakefulness that begins by quoting Byron’s “The Battle of Waterloo” is named for a poem by this other poet. In one poem, this poet wrote about the King and Queen of the pelicans hosting a party on the banks of the Nile for their daughter Nell. A character created by this author gathers the bark of the Tangum tree to construct an apparatus with a lamp inside after being spurned by a girl with (*) sky-blue hands and green hair. In another poem, this poet wrote about a group of creatures that, despite reservation from their friends, “went to sea in a Sieve.” This author of “The Dong with A Luminous Nose” and “The Jumblies” also created a pair of characters that eat “mince and slices of quince” with a “runcible spoon” after going to sea in a “pea-green boat.” For 10 points, name this Victorian nonsense poet who wrote “The Owl and the Pussycat.”
tossups.question_sanitized: A John Ashbury cento from his collection Wakefulness that begins by quoting Byron's "The Battle of Waterloo" is named for a poem by this other poet. In one poem, this poet wrote about the King and Queen of the pelicans hosting a party on the banks of the Nile for their daughter Nell. A character created by this author gathers the bark of the Tangum tree to construct an apparatus with a lamp inside after being spurned by a girl with (*) sky-blue hands and green hair. In another poem, this poet wrote about a group of creatures that, despite reservation from their friends, "went to sea in a Sieve." This author of "The Dong with A Luminous Nose" and "The Jumblies" also created a pair of characters that eat "mince and slices of quince" with a "runcible spoon" after going to sea in a "pea-green boat." For 10 points, name this Victorian nonsense poet who wrote "The Owl and the Pussycat."

bonuses.leadin: A woman who married into this family maintained a multi-decade correspondence with an author who named his villa in Sanremo after her. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A woman who married into this family maintained a multi-decade correspondence with an author who named his villa in Sanremo after her. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this family into which the sisters Louisa and Emily married. Emily composed music for poems such as “Break, Break, Break,” written by her spouse with this family name.
This longtime correspondent of Emily Tennyson illustrated and composed music for many of Alfred’s poems. This author’s nonsense poems include “The Owl and the Pussy-Cat.”

Edward Lear composed a lullaby-like musical setting of this poem by Tennyson, which appears between cantos II and III of The Princess. This poem’s title phrase describes the “Wind of the western sea.”
parts_sanitized: Name this family into which the sisters Louisa and Emily married. Emily composed music for poems such as "Break, Break, Break," written by her spouse with this family name.
This longtime correspondent of Emily Tennyson illustrated and composed music for many of Alfred's poems. This author's nonsense poems include "The Owl and the Pussy-Cat."

Edward Lear composed a lullaby-like musical setting of this poem by Tennyson, which appears between cantos II and III of The Princess. This poem's title phrase describes the "Wind of the western sea."
answers_sanitized: Tennyson [or Emily Tennyson; or Alfred, Lord Tennyson]

bonuses.leadin: His most famous protagonist figures out the relationship between the odd pattern of footsteps he hears and the theft of some pearl-handled fish cutlery in “The Queer Feet”. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this creator of the priest-detective Father Brown who wrote of a policeman going undercover as an anarchist in The Man Who Was Thursday.
This erstwhile collaborator of G.K. Chesterton and fellow staunch Catholic detailed a trip he took from France across to Italy in The Path to Rome and wrote Cautionary Tales For Children.
This earlier poet of light verse for children wrote A Book of Nonsense and a poem in which the title creatures go to sea in a pea-green boat, “The Owl and the Pussycat”.

packet.name: Bellevue + Michigan A + Georgia Tech FINAL
